description: Locked reference outputs for ADR-009 parity validation
llama_cpp_invocation: llama-completion (NOT llama-cli) with manually-applied chat template; locked-commit b3d758750a llama-cli rejects --no-conversation per its own message and forces interactive mode otherwise
refresh_note: ADR-005 iter-220 2026-05-01: full vs-llama refresh at locked commit b3d758750a on M5 Max, system-quiet window. Both fixtures (*_llama.txt + *_hf2q.txt) now anchored to today's measurement on today's hardware. The frozen Apr 16 fixtures used llama-completion with a manually-applied chat template (NOT llama-cli with -st); the iter-219b operator-runbook draft incorrectly named llama-cli + --no-conversation, which the locked commit rejects at startup. Corrected runbook in docs/operator-llama-refresh-runbook.md. Both sides verified deterministic across runs (sourdough byte-identical across two 1000-token captures). Cross-ADR argmax drift on long prompts (179 / 108 byte common-prefix vs prior 3094 / 700 floors) confirmed mathematical, not noise — root cause lives in independent kernel implementations (hf2q mlx-native vs llama.cpp ggml-metal) producing identically-coherent but argmax-divergent text on tight token decisions. Per ADR-005 close-out: vs-llama floors anchored to MEASURED common-prefix to detect future regressions in hf2q while acknowledging cross-implementation drift as the structural baseline. Investigation of the divergent-token math is ADR-015 territory (see project_w5b22_hf2q_exhausted_remaining_in_mul_mm_id memory).
divergence_note: Both sourdough and sliding_wrap diverge on word-choice argmax flips, NOT on coherence breakdown. Sourdough byte 179: hf2q='flavor and texture are superior' vs llama='result is a superior flavor and texture' (same meaning, different word order). Sliding_wrap byte 108: hf2q='"calculating" (solving math problems) to "processing"' vs llama='physical movement to electrical movement' (different metaphors, both coherent). Output coherence verified end-to-end on both sides — outputs are valid English completing the prompt. Cross-implementation argmax flips on tight tokens are inherent to independent kernel arithmetic; only ADR-015 mlx-native parity work could close them.

sourdough.divergence_context: ...slower, but the | hf2q='flavor and texture are superior.' / llama='result is a superior flavor and texture.'
sourdough.vs_llama_status: PASS — common_prefix 179 ≥ 179 floor (anchored to today's measurement)
sourdough.self_baseline_status: PASS — refreshed against HEAD 921dd23, byte-identical at HEAD 92f979a

short_hello.note: Divergence at byte 29 is EOS marker only — content identical. Locked-commit fresh-llama capture is byte-identical to frozen Apr 16 capture (46 bytes). Short prompts don't trigger the long-prompt argmax-cascade.
short_hello.self_baseline_status: PASS — byte-identical at all HEADs since Apr 16
